subject: hid-nintendo: support for Switch NES and SNES controllers.
author: Sorgelig
provenance_doc_ref: line 335, but grouped incorrectly with commits 2799f8b94 (N64, 2023-09-02) and b00a72159 (Mega Drive, 2023-09-04). This commit (2021-09-04) is a separate addition of NES/SNES support only.

impact.effect_if_absent: Without either the fork's or vanilla's support, Nintendo Switch Online SNES controller (dedicated PID 0x2017) fails to enumerate entirely — no input device created, completely non-functional. NES controllers (which reuse the standard Joy-Con R PID 0x2007, see device_ids) would be misidentified as a plain Joy-Con R: they would create an input device but the type-9/10 (0x09/0x0A) handshake byte would be unhandled, likely leaving buttons unmapped or misbehaving rather than cleanly failing. With vanilla 6.18.38's support present instead of the fork's: BOTH controller families work and are recognized, but for the plain NES controller specifically, the A and B buttons are SWAPPED relative to what the fork commit reports (see upstream.equivalence=partial) — a real, silent, button-mapping regression for anyone whose game-core default mappings assume the fork's A/B assignment.
device_ids: 057e:2017 (SNESCON — dedicated PID, own HID_USB_DEVICE + HID_BLUETOOTH_DEVICE id-table entries)

057e:2007 (NESL/NESR — NOT a distinct PID; shared with the ordinary Joy-Con R id-table entry, disambiguated purely by an internal ctlr_type handshake byte read at runtime, report->subcmd_reply.data[2] == 0x09/0x0A; no new hid_device_id entry is added for NES at all, in either the fork or vanilla)

userspace_coupling.interface: HID device enumeration / input event reporting via /dev/input/event*; Main_MiSTer player-number LED control via sysfs (joycon player LEDs); Main_MiSTer QUIRK_JOYCON combo-pairing logic (unintended secondary coupling — see main_mister_ref)
userspace_coupling.main_mister_ref: Main_MiSTer/input.cpp:2752-2765 (NOT ~1795 as originally cited — that line range is unrelated `get_map_*()` accessor code) — `else if (input[dev].vid == 0x057e && ((input[dev].pid & 0xFF00) == 0x2000)) { // nintendo switch controllers ... set_led(led_path, ":player1"/":player2"/... ) }`; this LED path matches SNESCON (0x2017) since 0x2017 & 0xFF00 == 0x2000, but does NOT match NES controllers (0x2007 & 0xFF00 == 0x2000 also matches — so NES DOES get this LED path too). ADDITIONAL coupling not in the original record: input.cpp:5331-5334 unconditionally sets `input[n].quirk = QUIRK_JOYCON` and `misc_flags = 1<<29` (JOYCON_RIGHT) for ANY device with vid=0x057e/pid=0x2007 — since NES controllers share this exact PID with the standard Joy-Con R, Main_MiSTer will attempt to treat a standalone NES controller as a right Joy-Con eligible for the JOYCON_COMBO left/right-pairing dance (input.cpp:4689 check_joycon(), :4740 process_joycon()). This is a real behavioral quirk of sharing the JOYCONR PID, present for BOTH the fork's and vanilla's implementation equally (not something this commit's disposition changes), but worth flagging since it was absent from the original coupling analysis.

license_provenance: GPL-2.0+; driver source is Daniel J. Ogorchock's hid-nintendo (originally out-of-tree, vendored by fork starting 2021-08-20, integrated upstream 2021-09-11 to v5.16, enhanced with NSO support 2023-12-04).
notes: GROUPING ERROR IN PROVENANCE DOC (line 335, confirmed still present): this commit is grouped with 2799f8b94 (N64 controllers, +2 years) and b00a72159 (Mega Drive, +2 years). They are separate additions at different times (e155f6a2f: 2021-09-04, 2799f8b94: 2023-09-02, b00a72159: 2023-09-04). Only e155f6a2f adds NES/SNES support. DISPOSITION: vanilla 6.18.38 via commit 94f18bb19945915fcdfd1903841020ef1b6af44a ('HID: nintendo: add support for nso controllers', Ryan McClelland, 2023-12-04) provides the same DEVICE coverage (same type enum values 0x09/0x0A/0x0B; same SNESCON id-table entries) with identical D-pad handling, identical rumble/IMU exclusion for NES/SNES (joycon_has_rumble()/joycon_has_imu() at hid-nintendo.c:746-767 exclude both types by construction, matching the fork's explicit skip), and IDENTICAL SNES button mapping (A→BTN_EAST, B→BTN_SOUTH, X→BTN_NORTH, Y→BTN_WEST, L→BTN_TL, R→BTN_TR, ZL→BTN_TL2, ZR→BTN_TR2, MINUS→BTN_SELECT, PLUS→BTN_START — verified byte-for-byte against snescon_button_mappings[] at hid-nintendo.c:446-458). BUT the standalone NES controller mapping is NOT identical: nescon_button_mappings[] at hid-nintendo.c:436-444 maps JC_BTN_A→BTN_SOUTH and JC_BTN_B→BTN_EAST, which is the REVERSE of the fork's shared A/B block (`input_report_key(dev, BTN_EAST, btns & JC_BTN_A); input_report_key(dev, BTN_SOUTH, btns & JC_BTN_B);` — verified via `git show` diff, applies unconditionally to BOTH nescon and snescon in the fork). This is a REAL, confirmed A/B swap for the plain NES controller only (not SNES) — SONNET-VERIFY caught the original record's 'byte-identical' grade as WRONG; per worker-instructions ('independent implementations are rarely byte-identical; check button-for-button') this is exactly the failure mode flagged. Independently corroborated: docs/patch-provenance.md §9.2 (lines 1343-1352, written for a DIFFERENT record, the 0015-hid-nintendo-nso-famicom.patch carry) already documents this exact swap: "The original reports 'A' as BTN_EAST and 'B' as BTN_SOUTH for all NSO controllers. Upstream 6.18 maps NES-family pads positionally instead — 'A' → BTN_SOUTH, 'B' → BTN_EAST ... Consequence: A and B are swapped relative to stock MiSTer" — this is a pre-existing, already-known consequence of taking upstream's hid-nintendo, just never linked back to THIS record before. Re-graded equivalence byte-identical → partial: SNES is functionally byte-identical, NES has a confirmed behavioral divergence in the A/B assignment (everything else — D-pad, L/R, Select/Start, rumble/IMU exclusion, device probing — matches). Also corrected: device_ids expanded (SNES has a real dedicated PID 0x2017; NES has NO dedicated PID at all, it rides on the pre-existing Joy-Con R entry 0x2007, disambiguated only by a runtime handshake byte — re-derived from hid-ids.h and the nintendo_hid_devices[] id table, which the original record's device_ids field did not capture); main_mister_ref citation corrected from a non-existent ~1795 to the actual LED logic at input.cpp:2752-2765, plus a newly-found QUIRK_JOYCON/combo-pairing coupling at input.cpp:5331-5334 that the original coupling analysis missed. Not carried (no 00xx patch file exists for this specific commit; the LATER, SEPARATE 0015-hid-nintendo-nso-famicom.patch carries Famicom support and inherits this same A/B convention deliberately, per patch-provenance.md §9.2). FAILURE MODE IS SILENT either way — no probe error, just missing/altered buttons. sonnet-verified: confirmed vanilla-SHA ancestry (`git merge-base --is-ancestor 94f18bb19945915fcdfd1903841020ef1b6af44a v6.18.38` holds), disposition, and grouping-error finding; corrected equivalence (byte-identical→partial), device_ids (added 057e:2007 nuance for NES), and main_mister_ref (wrong line ~1795 → actual 2752-2765, plus added the QUIRK_JOYCON coupling at 5331-5334). | RESOLVED (user carry decision 2026-07-14/15 (see silent-regressions.md + git d2f2791 follow-ups)): disposition stays dropped-upstream (controllers supported upstream); the A/B assignment divergence is restored to stock via 0034 (also aligns 0015's Famicom tables).
